events.thought: Bootstrap the foundation: database schema + auth, Stripe models, basic React setup, then test the auth critical path.

assignments.spec: Create tables for users, tenants, subscriptions (per-seat pricing model), invoices, line_items, and stripe_events. Include indexes on tenant_id and user_id.
Implement signup, login, JWT token generation/verification with bcrypt password hashing. Export authenticate middleware.
Initialize Stripe SDK client, export functions for creating customers, subscriptions, and invoices. Handle Stripe API errors cleanly.
Create React dashboard page showing list of tenants and their subscriptions in Tailwind cards. Stub data for now, no API calls yet.

events.spec: Create tables for users, tenants, subscriptions (per-seat pricing model), invoices, line_items, and stripe_events. Include indexes on tenant_id and user_id.
Implement signup, login, JWT token generation/verification with bcrypt password hashing. Export authenticate middleware.
Initialize Stripe SDK client, export functions for creating customers, subscriptions, and invoices. Handle Stripe API errors cleanly.
Create React dashboard page showing list of tenants and their subscriptions in Tailwind cards. Stub data for now, no API calls yet.

events.thought: Build out the core API endpoints (auth, tenants, subscriptions), webhook handler, and test coverage for critical paths. Then finalize the dashboard.

assignments.spec: POST /auth/signup, /auth/login endpoints with input validation, JWT token response, error handling for duplicate email/invalid creds
GET /tenants (list all for admin), POST /tenants (create new org), middleware to verify auth and tenant ownership for tenant-scoped routes
Handle stripe webhook events (invoice.created, invoice.paid, customer.subscription.updated), update subscription status/invoice records in DB
Jest+supertest tests for signup (valid/duplicate email), login (valid/invalid creds), JWT token validation, auth middleware protection

events.spec: POST /auth/signup, /auth/login endpoints with input validation, JWT token response, error handling for duplicate email/invalid creds
GET /tenants (list all for admin), POST /tenants (create new org), middleware to verify auth and tenant ownership for tenant-scoped routes
Handle stripe webhook events (invoice.created, invoice.paid, customer.subscription.updated), update subscription status/invoice records in DB
Jest+supertest tests for signup (valid/duplicate email), login (valid/invalid creds), JWT token validation, auth middleware protection

events.thought: Need subscriptions API, invoice management, webhook integration into routes, and more test coverage. Then dashboard components for subscriptions.

assignments.spec: POST /subscriptions (create per-seat subscription linked to tenant), GET /subscriptions/:tenantId (list), PUT /subscriptions/:id (update seat count), DELETE /subscriptions/:id (cancel)
GET /invoices/:tenantId (list invoices), POST /invoices (manual invoice creation with line items), GET /invoices/:id (detail with line items)
POST /webhooks/stripe (route that verifies Stripe signature, delegates to webhookHandler, returns 200)
React component showing subscription detail (name, seats, pricing), update seat count form, cancel button, list recent invoices

events.spec: POST /subscriptions (create per-seat subscription linked to tenant), GET /subscriptions/:tenantId (list), PUT /subscriptions/:id (update seat count), DELETE /subscriptions/:id (cancel)
GET /invoices/:tenantId (list invoices), POST /invoices (manual invoice creation with line items), GET /invoices/:id (detail with line items)
POST /webhooks/stripe (route that verifies Stripe signature, delegates to webhookHandler, returns 200)
React component showing subscription detail (name, seats, pricing), update seat count form, cancel button, list recent invoices

events.thought: We have auth, tenants, subscriptions, invoices, and webhook routes. Missing: database schema/migrations, tenant isolation middleware, admin dashboard layout, and critical path tests. Start with DB schema so backend can actually persist data.

assignments.spec: Define Postgres schema: users (id, email, password_hash, created_at), tenants (id, name, stripe_customer_id), subscriptions (id, tenant_id, stripe_subscription_id, seats, monthly_price, status), invoices (id, tenant_id, stripe_invoice_id, amount, status, line_items). Include migrations or raw SQL.
Middleware that extracts tenant_id from request (query param or JWT), verifies user belongs to tenant, attaches tenant context to req.tenant. Protects tenant-scoped routes.

React admin page with two tabs: Tenants list (table: name, stripe_id, created_at, action buttons), Subscriptions list (table: tenant name, seats, price, status, invoice link). Use Tailwind + dummy API calls.
Jest+supertest: test POST /subscriptions (create with valid tenant), GET /subscriptions/:tenantId (list), verify tenant isolation (user can't access other tenant's subs). Mock Stripe calls.

events.spec: Define Postgres schema: users (id, email, password_hash, created_at), tenants (id, name, stripe_customer_id), subscriptions (id, tenant_id, stripe_subscription_id, seats, monthly_price, status), invoices (id, tenant_id, stripe_invoice_id, amount, status, line_items). Include migrations or raw SQL.
Middleware that extracts tenant_id from request (query param or JWT), verifies user belongs to tenant, attaches tenant context to req.tenant. Protects tenant-scoped routes.

React admin page with two tabs: Tenants list (table: name, stripe_id, created_at, action buttons), Subscriptions list (table: tenant name, seats, price, status, invoice link). Use Tailwind + dummy API calls.
Jest+supertest: test POST /subscriptions (create with valid tenant), GET /subscriptions/:tenantId (list), verify tenant isolation (user can't access other tenant's subs). Mock Stripe calls.

events.thought: We have routes, schema, auth middleware, and some tests. Now we need: (1) database initialization + connection pool, (2) user auth routes (register/login), (3) invoice generation logic, (4) Stripe webhook handlers that actually process events, (5) more test coverage. Start with DB setup and auth routes to unblock frontend integration.

assignments.spec: Create Postgres connection pool (node-postgres), export query function, add connection error handling.
POST /auth/register (email, password, create user + initial tenant), POST /auth/login (email, password, return JWT token).
Helper functions: createStripeCustomer(tenantId), createStripeSubscription(customerId, priceId, seats), handleInvoicePayment(invoiceId, stripeInvoiceId).
Jest+supertest: test POST /auth/register (success, duplicate email), POST /auth/login (valid/invalid credentials, JWT returned).

events.spec: Create Postgres connection pool (node-postgres), export query function, add connection error handling.
POST /auth/register (email, password, create user + initial tenant), POST /auth/login (email, password, return JWT token).
Helper functions: createStripeCustomer(tenantId), createStripeSubscription(customerId, priceId, seats), handleInvoicePayment(invoiceId, stripeInvoiceId).
Jest+supertest: test POST /auth/register (success, duplicate email), POST /auth/login (valid/invalid credentials, JWT returned).

events.thought: We have auth and db setup. Now build core subscription creation, invoice generation, and Stripe webhook handling to complete the billing loop.

assignments.spec: POST /subscriptions (tenant_id, stripe_customer_id, plan_id, seat_count); create subscription record in DB and call stripeService.createSubscription(). GET /subscriptions/:id to fetch subscription + status.
GET /invoices?tenant_id=X (list invoices for tenant with line items); POST /invoices (internal: create invoice from subscription); helper to calculate usage-based charges.
POST /webhooks/stripe (verify signature, handle invoice.payment_succeeded + customer.subscription.updated events, update invoice status + subscription status in DB).
Jest+supertest: test GET /invoices (list invoices for tenant), test invoice line items, verify invoice status after webhook event.

events.spec: POST /subscriptions (tenant_id, stripe_customer_id, plan_id, seat_count); create subscription record in DB and call stripeService.createSubscription(). GET /subscriptions/:id to fetch subscription + status.
GET /invoices?tenant_id=X (list invoices for tenant with line items); POST /invoices (internal: create invoice from subscription); helper to calculate usage-based charges.
POST /webhooks/stripe (verify signature, handle invoice.payment_succeeded + customer.subscription.updated events, update invoice status + subscription status in DB).
Jest+supertest: test GET /invoices (list invoices for tenant), test invoice line items, verify invoice status after webhook event.

events.thought: Backend foundation solid (auth, subscriptions, invoices). Need: (1) main Express app wiring everything up, (2) frontend admin dashboard to visualize tenants/subscriptions, (3) integration tests for webhook flow.

assignments.spec: Create Express app: wire up auth/subscriptions/invoices routes, stripe webhook route, error middleware, CORS for frontend
Helper: generateInvoice(subscriptionId, lineItems[]), calculateTotal(), store in DB with status 'draft' or 'sent'
React component: list all tenants (GET /tenants), show tenant details + active subscriptions, display recent invoices, Tailwind styled
Jest+supertest: mock Stripe webhook signature, test POST /webhooks/stripe handles invoice.payment_succeeded, verify invoice marked paid

events.spec: Create Express app: wire up auth/subscriptions/invoices routes, stripe webhook route, error middleware, CORS for frontend
Helper: generateInvoice(subscriptionId, lineItems[]), calculateTotal(), store in DB with status 'draft' or 'sent'
React component: list all tenants (GET /tenants), show tenant details + active subscriptions, display recent invoices, Tailwind styled
Jest+supertest: mock Stripe webhook signature, test POST /webhooks/stripe handles invoice.payment_succeeded, verify invoice marked paid

events.thought: We have auth, subscriptions, invoices, webhooks, and a basic dashboard. Now we need: (1) tenant CRUD endpoints, (2) subscription list/management UI, (3) payment method handling, (4) critical path tests for auth+subscription flow. Start with tenant endpoints and subscription UI to unblock the dashboard.

assignments.spec: POST /tenants (create org, require auth), GET /tenants (list for user), GET /tenants/:id (get org details). Use middleware to verify user owns tenant.
React component: list subscriptions for selected tenant, show plan/status/next_billing_date, buttons to upgrade/cancel. Call GET /subscriptions?tenant_id=X.
Helper: createStripeCustomer(tenantId, email), attachPaymentMethod(customerId, paymentMethodId), listPaymentMethods(customerId).
End-to-end Jest+supertest: register user → create tenant → create subscription (with mock Stripe) → verify invoice generated → check webhook updates status.

events.spec: POST /tenants (create org, require auth), GET /tenants (list for user), GET /tenants/:id (get org details). Use middleware to verify user owns tenant.
React component: list subscriptions for selected tenant, show plan/status/next_billing_date, buttons to upgrade/cancel. Call GET /subscriptions?tenant_id=X.
Helper: createStripeCustomer(tenantId, email), attachPaymentMethod(customerId, paymentMethodId), listPaymentMethods(customerId).
End-to-end Jest+supertest: register user → create tenant → create subscription (with mock Stripe) → verify invoice generated → check webhook updates status.

events.thought: We have auth, tenants, subscriptions scaffolded, and Stripe integration started. Need: (1) invoice routes + DB schema, (2) complete Stripe webhook handlers, (3) frontend invoice view, (4) auth middleware + user table tests. Start with invoice routes and database schema to unblock the rest.

assignments.spec: SQL schema: users (id, email, password_hash), tenants (id, org_name, created_at), subscriptions (id, tenant_id, stripe_subscription_id, status, pricing_model), invoices (id, subscription_id, amount, status, created_at), line_items (id, invoice_id, description, quantity, unit_price). Include indexes on foreign keys and tenant_id.

Stripe webhook handler: on customer.subscription.updated → update subscription status in DB, on invoice.payment_succeeded → mark invoice as paid, validate webhook signature using STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET.
React component: display invoices for selected subscription (GET /subscriptions/:id/invoices), show line items table, invoice status badge (paid/unpaid), download PDF link stub.
Jest+supertest: test POST /subscriptions/:id/invoices (create invoice), GET /subscriptions/:id/invoices (list), verify line items match, test invoice status transitions (unpaid→paid).

events.spec: SQL schema: users (id, email, password_hash), tenants (id, org_name, created_at), subscriptions (id, tenant_id, stripe_subscription_id, status, pricing_model), invoices (id, subscription_id, amount, status, created_at), line_items (id, invoice_id, description, quantity, unit_price). Include indexes on foreign keys and tenant_id.

Stripe webhook handler: on customer.subscription.updated → update subscription status in DB, on invoice.payment_succeeded → mark invoice as paid, validate webhook signature using STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET.
React component: display invoices for selected subscription (GET /subscriptions/:id/invoices), show line items table, invoice status badge (paid/unpaid), download PDF link stub.
Jest+supertest: test POST /subscriptions/:id/invoices (create invoice), GET /subscriptions/:id/invoices (list), verify line items match, test invoice status transitions (unpaid→paid).

events.thought: System is missing core subscription creation + invoice generation logic, and admin dashboard routing. Need: (1) backend subscription controller to create/list subs, (2) invoice service to generate line items, (3) frontend router + layout for admin, (4) test coverage for webhook-triggered invoice creation.

assignments.spec: POST /subscriptions (create per-seat subscription: tenantId, seats, pricePerSeat, interval), GET /subscriptions (list for tenant), GET /subscriptions/:id (details). Sync with Stripe.
generateInvoice(subscriptionId, lineItems[]) → create Invoice + InvoiceLineItem rows, return invoice object. Called by webhook + manual POST.
Root admin layout: React Router (list tenants, select tenant → show SubscriptionManager + InvoiceViewer as tabs). Tailwind nav + responsive.
Jest+supertest: POST /webhooks/stripe (simulate customer.subscription.updated) → verify invoice auto-generated, check db rows, assert invoice email queued.

events.spec: POST /subscriptions (create per-seat subscription: tenantId, seats, pricePerSeat, interval), GET /subscriptions (list for tenant), GET /subscriptions/:id (details). Sync with Stripe.
generateInvoice(subscriptionId, lineItems[]) → create Invoice + InvoiceLineItem rows, return invoice object. Called by webhook + manual POST.
Root admin layout: React Router (list tenants, select tenant → show SubscriptionManager + InvoiceViewer as tabs). Tailwind nav + responsive.
Jest+supertest: POST /webhooks/stripe (simulate customer.subscription.updated) → verify invoice auto-generated, check db rows, assert invoice email queued.

events.thought: System needs: auth middleware + tenant routes, Stripe customer sync on tenant creation, subscription listing endpoint, and critical path tests. Start with auth & tenant CRUD.

assignments.spec: JWT auth middleware: verify token, attach userId/tenantId to req.user; also export signToken(userId, tenantId) and middleware chain for protected routes
POST /tenants (create org: name, userId as owner), GET /tenants (list user's orgs), GET /tenants/:id (fetch one with member count). All protected.
createStripeCustomer(tenantId, tenantName) → call Stripe API, save customer_id to tenants table. syncCustomerMetadata(customerId, tenantId, userId)
Jest+supertest: test signup, login (return JWT), POST /tenants, GET /tenants, verify auth guard blocks unauth requests

events.spec: JWT auth middleware: verify token, attach userId/tenantId to req.user; also export signToken(userId, tenantId) and middleware chain for protected routes
POST /tenants (create org: name, userId as owner), GET /tenants (list user's orgs), GET /tenants/:id (fetch one with member count). All protected.
createStripeCustomer(tenantId, tenantName) → call Stripe API, save customer_id to tenants table. syncCustomerMetadata(customerId, tenantId, userId)
Jest+supertest: test signup, login (return JWT), POST /tenants, GET /tenants, verify auth guard blocks unauth requests

events.thought: We have auth, tenants, subscriptions, invoices, and Stripe customers. Now we need: (1) invoice listing/retrieval endpoints, (2) Stripe webhook signature verification, (3) admin dashboard pages for subscriptions & invoices, (4) end-to-end test for complete billing flow.

assignments.spec: GET /invoices (list by tenantId with pagination), GET /invoices/:id (retrieve single invoice with line items). Require auth middleware.
Middleware to verify Stripe webhook signature using raw body + webhook secret. Extract event and pass to handler.
Display subscriptions table (tenantId, status, seats, price) with create/update/cancel actions. Use React hooks + API calls.
E2E flow: signup user → create tenant → create subscription → invoice generated → retrieve invoice. Assert all state transitions.
